Definition (EN): Annotations for attaching metadata to a model, including comments and textual representations (see 7.4).
Context/Example (EN): SysML directly uses the following concepts from KerML: Annotations for attaching metadata to a model, including comments and textual representations (see 7.4).

Definition (EN): An annotating element is an element that is used to provide additional information about other elements. An annotation is a relationship between an annotating element and an annotated element that is being described.
Context/Example (EN): An annotating element is an element that is used to provide additional information about other elements.

Definition (EN): An annotation is a relationship between an annotating element and an annotated element that is being described.
Context/Example (EN): An annotating element can annotate multiple annotated elements, and each element can have multiple annotations.

Definition (EN): An annotation is a relationship between an annotating element and an annotated element that is being described.
Context/Example (EN): An annotating element can annotate multiple annotated elements, and each element can have multiple annotations.

Definition (EN): A comment is one kind of annotating element that is used to provide textual descriptions about other elements.
Context/Example (EN): A comment is one kind of annotating element that is used to provide textual descriptions about other elements.

Equivalent (JA): ドキュメンテーション
Definition (EN): Documentation is a distinguished kind of comment used to document the annotated element. Documentation comments always annotate a single element, which is their owning element.
Context/Example (EN): Documentation is a distinguished kind of comment used to document the annotated element.

Definition (EN): A textual representation is an annotating element whose textual body provides a representation of the annotated element in a specifically named language.
Context/Example (EN): A textual representation is an annotating element whose textual body provides a representation of the annotated element in a specifically named language.

Equivalent (JA): ユーザー定義メタデータ
Definition (EN): It is also possible to annotate elements with user-defined metadata, allowing both syntactic and semantic extension of SysML.
Context/Example (EN): It is also possible to annotate elements with user-defined metadata, allowing both syntactic and semantic extension of SysML.

Definition (EN): The full declaration of a comment begins with the keyword comment, optionally followed by a short name and/or name (see 7.2).
Context/Example (EN): The full declaration of a comment begins with the keyword comment, optionally followed by a short name and/or name (see 7.2).

Definition (EN): One or more qualified names of annotated elements for the comment, separated by commas, are then given after the keyword about, indicating that the comment has annotation relationships to each of the identified elements.
Context/Example (EN): comment Comment1 about A, B /* This is the comment body text. */

Definition (EN): The body of the comment is written lexically as text between /* and */ delimiters.
Context/Example (EN): The body of the comment is written lexically as text between /* and */ delimiters.

Definition (EN): A locale can also be specified for a comment, using the keyword locale followed by the locale string, placed immediately before the comment body (whether or not the comment keyword is used).
Context/Example (EN): comment C_US_English locale "en_US" /* This is US English comment text */

Definition (EN): A documentation comment is notated similarly to a regular comment, but using the keyword doc rather than comment.
Context/Example (EN): A documentation comment is notated similarly to a regular comment, but using the keyword doc rather than comment.

Definition (EN): A textual representation is notated similarly to a regular comment, but with the keyword rep used instead of comment.
Context/Example (EN): calc def square { in attribute x : ScalarValues::Real; return :ScalarValues::Real; rep square language "Python" /* * def square(x:float) -> float: * return x**2 */ }

Definition (EN): A textual representation declaration must also specify the language as a literal string following the keyword language.
Context/Example (EN): action def setX(c : C, newX : Real) { language "alf" /* c.x = newX; * WriteLine("Set new x"); */ }

Equivalent (JA): 意味的に「不透明な」要素
Definition (EN): An element with no other definition than a textual representation in a language other than SysML is essentially a semantically "opaque" element specified in the other language.
Context/Example (EN): An element with no other definition than a textual representation in a language other than SysML is essentially a semantically "opaque" element specified in the other language.

Equivalent (JA): メタデータフィーチャ
Definition (EN): A metadata feature is syntactically a feature (see [KerML, 7.3.4]) that is typed by a single metaclass, which is a kind of structure (see [KerML, 7.4.4]), with implicit multiplicity 1..1.
Context/Example (EN): Specific kinds of annotating elements include comments and textual representations (see [KerML, 7.2.4.2] and [KerML, 7.2.4.3]). A further kind of annotating element for user-defined metadata is defined in the Kernel layer (see [KerML, 7.4.13]).

Definition (EN): Metadata is additional information on elements of a model that does not have any instance-level semantics (in the sense described in [KerML, 7.3.1]).
Context/Example (EN): Metadata is additional information on elements of a model that does not have any instance-level semantics (in the sense described in [KerML, 7.3.1]).

Definition (EN): A metaclass is declared like a structure (see [KerML, 7.4.4]), but using the keyword metaclass.
Context/Example (EN): metaclass SecurityRelated;

Definition (EN): A metadata feature is declared using the keyword metadata (or the symbol @), optionally followed by a short name and/or name, followed by the keyword typed by (or the symbol :) and the qualified name of exactly one metaclass.
Context/Example (EN): metadata SecurityAnnotation : SecurityRelated about SecurityDesign;

Equivalent (JA): ユーザー定義キーワード
Definition (EN): A user-defined keyword is a (possibly qualified) metaclass name or short name preceded by the symbol #.
Context/Example (EN): #command behavior Save;
